intro: Oxydectes noronhae, also known as the Noronha's grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxydectes noronhae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been found to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powder supplements, which are used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
